item.title: Re: More dangerous?
item.content:encoded:
Remember, Object Class is solely based on how difficult it is to contain. There could be an item that you just have to touch it and the world Would explode, but if you can just stick it in a Storage locker and forget about it it’s still just a Safe SCP.

You can place this little figurine in a Storage locker and it will never escape so it can still be considered Safe.

item.title: Re: More dangerous?
item.content:encoded: Well you're right. I know this is an old comment but it's just too dangerous and in the wrong hands it would be a decent XK Scenario

item.title: More dangerous?
item.content:encoded:
Shouldn't this be Euclid as storage concern are greater than ordinary Safe items?
A GoI with apocalyptic motives could "infect" 100 people and then blow up Dubrovnik, essentially generating a meganuke, if that's even enough of a word.

Ok, so if my math isn't totally bonkers; if the 15th person was equivalent to 1 ton of TNT and the $power of 2$ rule is true, another 84 explosions would result in an explosion of 19342813113834066795298816 tons of TNT. That's 19342813113834067 gigatons of TNT (rounded up). Not sure the solar system can handle that, the K-T event that ended the dinosaurs was estimated to 1000 gigatons. So… XK-Class End-of-the-World Scenario anyone?

+1 for great quirkiness! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has a classic SCP weirdness to it that really just works. Well done.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not gonna’ lie, I was expecting an AWCY name drop.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As everyone else is saying, this is delightfully charming, and nicely explored while still feeling there could be more yet to discover.

I particularly liked the interview log, especially for this line:

SCP-3037-A: The same as usual. Rocky, ancient, humid, warm today.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dr David Brajkovic, this is really not the ideal place to advertise your drafts. This thread should be about this article (SCP-3037) or things related to it. Advertising a draft unrelated/tangentially related to the article in question is not optimal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been in Dubrovink on the holidays, loved it and I love this SCP. Quirky, interasting yet so simple.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quirky as fuck, and you carried the idea to an excellent conclusion.

Chemical analysis of SCP-3037 consistently indicates that it is not made of marble.

If there's one line that got me to upvote, it's this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Having had a holiday in Dubrovnik in 1988, this SCP has a particular real-world resonance with me.

And shows that you don't have to have 99 pages of interviews and exploration logs to write a good SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was delightful. Goofy without being forced, logically thought-out, and an incident log that conveyed unexpected, but fully sensible, results that expand on the nature of the anomaly. Great work.
This is weird, quirky, simple, and interesting. This has an essence of charm that more recent articles lack… enjoyable read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My fourth SCP! The image is my own; I took it myself.
